programmes.show_description: The Gloria Scott. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read in which an old friend of Sherlock's comes to him for help regarding a visit his father received by seaman from his past.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 1. 1974. Stars David Ashford, William Mervyn & William Simons. Irene Rutland is in court facing charges of theft, criminal damage and wounding a police officer.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 2. 1974. Stars Katharine Blake, David Troughton & Sara Clee. Linda Brightson accuses the defendant of taking her handbag but Mrs Rutland denies the charge.
The Woman Least Likely - Part 3. 1974. Stars Griffith Jones, William Marlowe & David Ashford. Bryan Hodge admits that after the death of Irene's husband, she began to drink alcohol after work.
1992. Crime drama with Bernard Hill and Norman Wisdom. Former petty thief and safecracker Arthur is the 'brains' behind 'The Organisation' but he wants out.
1963. Who Killed Purity Mather? Directed Walter Grauman. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. A practicing witch sends Amos a message predicting her murder with a list of five suspects.
1960. Rabbit's Fang. Stars Michael Ansara, Don Dubbins & Constance Ford. Sam becomes involved in a conflict with a gunfighter and a greedy husband.
1954. Directed by Wolf Rilla and starring Jimmy Hanley, Rona Anderson and Lionel Jeffries. A local reporter discovers a group of enemy agents, but will the story get him promotion?
1972. Comedy. Directed by John Robins. Stars Hylda Baker, Jimmy Jewel & Edward Malin. After their father's death, two siblings inherit a decrepit pickle factory and attempt to keep it afloat.
1975. Family. Directed by David Eady. Stars Stephen Garlick, Ray Barrett & Robin Askwith. A group of children find themselves held hostage by two escaped convicts from a nearby prison.
1964. Comedy. Director: Jeremy Summers. Stars Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Melvyn Hayes. After pulling off the smallest train robbery, Little Walter and his crew decide to get out of London.
Candidate for Murder. 1961. Directed by David Villiers. Stars Michael Gough, Erika Remberg & John Justin. Kersten, a professional killer, is brought in by a jealous husband to kill his wife.
1962. Crime drama starring Richard Todd, Robert Morley, Dudley Sutton & Jess Conrad. When a nightwatchman is murdered, four young men are put on trial. Cleverly directed by Sidney J. Furie.
It's time to move with Vince Taylor as he performs Whole Lotta Twisting.
Intent to Defraud. 1960. Stars John Ireland, Joseph O'Conor & Philip Latham. When an aging playwright dies of a seizure, Hunter investigates to discover whether it was an accident or a murder.
1961. War. Directed by Ernest Morris. Stars Dermot Walsh & Patrick McAlinney. Major Bell and a ragtag team undertake a dangerous mission to destroy a bridge of strategic importance to the enemy.
1945. In this charming short video Charlie Chester promotes the importance of orange juice for children under five.
1952. Directed by Maclean Rogers and starring John Bentley, Patricia Dainton and Peter Gawthorne. Follows Temple's efforts to aid the police in solving a series of gruesome murders.
Full Remission. 1962. Stars Stratford Johns, Diane Aubrey, Douglas Cummings & Yootha Joyce. Two people are robbed at gun point, Chief Inspector Barlow is suspicious about the cash that was stolen.
Caroline Munro is back this week to introduce the next episode of Supernatural, in which Lady Sybil is seeing ghosts!
Lady Sybil. 1977. Stars Cathleen Nesbitt, Denholm Elliott & John Osborne. Lady Sybil is frightened of a Phantom, she wonders if the ghost of her dead husband haunts the grounds.
Prepare for a night of mad scientists, monsters and murder because next up, Caroline Munro introduces a 1959 horror film: The Manster.
1959. Horror. Director: George P. Breakston. Stars Peter Dyneley, Jane Hylton & Tetsu Nakamura. A journalist is given a mysterious injection that turns him into a murderous monster.
As the end of the night approaches, Caroline Munro is here to give you an insight into what is to come next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Czech Mate. 1984. Stars Susan George, Patrick Mower & Peter Vaughan. Vicky goes to Prague with her ex-husband but soon finds herself alone, penniless and caught up in a murder.
1977. Thriller. Directed by Robert Aldrich. Stars Burt Lancaster, Richard Widmark & Roscoe Lee Browne. An imprisoned rogue Air Force general seizes a nuclear silo and threatens to start WWIII.
1954. Romance. Directed by Paul Dickson. Stars Griffth Jones, Kathleen Byron & Hugh Williams. A cynical sculptor becomes inspired by a ballerina he meets, but this only enrages her jealous lover.
Romney Marsh/Pumpkins. 1986. Jack looks back at a sunny visit to Kent when he tried to photograph a 'flying frog' on Romney Marsh, and he visits a village pumpkin fair.
1946. Thriller. Director: Gordon Douglas. Stars Morgan Conway, Dick Wessel & Esther Howard. Dick Tracy is on the trail of the rampaging arch nemesis, Cueball.
The Stake. 1974. Stars Roberta Tovey, Brian Coburn & Simon Cuff. Lady Ann is put on the stand to determine whether she is guilty of witchcraft, but will Black Arrow return and save the day?
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. When the two strange boys arrive in his realm, King Nerada worries about the fulfilment of ancient prophecies.
The Magic Book. 1956. Stars William Russell, Ronald Leigh-Hunt & Cyril Smith. Sir Lancelot helps to keep safe an Abbey and the occupants from invaders.
The Duo Defy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze gives Professor Isaacson an ultimatum: meet his demands, or he will begin to freeze the entire country.
Outcasts of Paradise Valley. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger & Dale Evans. Unable to find a job, two young men decide on a life of crime. Their first victims are an elderly couple.
Big Ben Strikes Again. 1967. Stars Sylvia Anderson, Donald Gray & Janna Hill. The Mysterons announce they intend to destroy London and a truck carrying an atomic device goes missing in the city.
The Young Widow. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Having escaped from Gessler's troops, Tell and his wife are given refuge by the beautiful Countess von Marheim.
The Hand of the Hawk. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Anthony Dawson & Sid James. Tempest tries to solve the murder of a crew member from another ship. A mysterious clue may just hold the answer.
The Traitor. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. King John fails to gather money to release Richard, some Lords raise the money. Robin must secretly get it out the country.
1963. Family. Directed by Colin Bell. Stars Christopher Brett, Shirley Joy & Peter Butterworth. A group of children attempt to retrieve their toy plane from the top of an abandoned tower.
24 Hours. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Robert Arden & Joy Owen. In Piccadilly, Maguire spots Caldwell, a criminal he once arrested in Canada; he has a hunch that he is up to no good and follows him.

programmes.show_name: The Ship That Died of Shame
programmes.show_description: (AKA PT Raiders) 1955. Thriller. Director Basil Dearden. Stars Richard Attenborough, George Baker and Bill Owen. A trio of ex-servicemen begin smuggling black market items into post-war Britain.
1945. A short documentary about football. This film explores everything football such as teaching school children football techniques, scouting the next professional players and nationwide matches!
Dutchman's Reef. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Leonard Teale. Mrs. Culbert summons Cobb to search for her missing son but he is not interested until they come to an agreement.
1956. Drama. Directed by Charles Frend. Stars Jack Hawkins, John Stratton, Dorothy Alison & Michael Brooke. The sleuths of Scotland Yard try to solve a series of burglaries.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life High, Wide and Faster
programmes.show_description: 1963. A captivating look at the plans for closer co-ordination between our roads, railways and coastal shipping.
The Trap. 1962. Stars Rupert Davies, Sonia Dresdel & Jacqueline Hill. Maigret creates a trap to capture a serial killer who is targeting women in the streets of Montmartre.
1974. Thriller. Directed by Ronald Neame. Stars Jon Voight, Maximillian Schell & Maria Schell. A reporter tries to track down a number of SS officers who mysteriously vanished after the war.
1961. Crime. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars John Hewer, Ann Lynn & Jean Muir. A dancer collapses and dies on stage at the famous Flamingo Club leading her husband to delve into the investigation.
